live_path.role: CLI entrypoint parses --owner and forwards it into the service layer.
Orchestrates owner resolution, base-record construction, routing-key derivation, and payload emission.
Derives effective owner and owner_source from explicit CLI input or config defaults.
Builds the base record with name, status, and effective owner only.
Builds routing_key from the already-resolved effective owner and item name.
Emits the final payload by copying the base record and adding owner_source and routing_key.

owner_source.rule: Returns "explicit" when owner is provided and non-blank; otherwise returns "default" with the configured fallback owner.

routing_key.rule: Slugify owner and name, then join as '<owner>:<name>'.

test_observations.observation: Direct service call with owner='pm-oncall' yields owner_source='explicit' and routing_key='pm-oncall:launch-checklist'.
Missing owner falls back to config/defaults.json and yields owner_source='default' with a routing key based on the default owner.
CLI path passes the explicit owner through to the emitted payload without any storage-side derivation.
Runtime output emitted owner='pm-oncall', owner_source='explicit', and routing_key='pm-oncall:launch-checklist'.

rejected_decoys.reason: Unused helper; no repo-local caller places it on the live request path.
Legacy helper; sync_app/service.py::sync_item calls make_record instead.
Legacy helper; sync_app/service.py::sync_item imports build_routing_key from sync_app/serializer.py.
Its storage-origin and pre-CLI-routing_key hypotheses conflict with the implemented flow and tests.
